reasoning: The 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship was pre-recorded in August 2025. Although Stephen Chidwick was part of the 64-player field, multiple poker and gambling news outlets reported in October 2025 that a massive leak on Polymarket essentially spoiled the results of the tournament. The betting activity overwhelmingly pointed to Sam Soverel as the winner, with his implied odds reaching over 90%-99% before the broadcast. Since this event was pre-recorded and there are credible leaks pointing directly to another player, the probability of Chidwick winning is extremely low. I estimate a 2% chance just in case the leak is some elaborate ruse or misunderstanding, but the evidence heavily favors another winner.
